issue_title: compiled with Address Sanitizer?
issue_body:
I don't know whether this should be reported here - please let me know where to go if not.

When installing requests-kerberos (with pip in a Python 3.5.2 installation in macOS 10.12 Sierra), a file "kerberos.cpython-35m-darwin.so" gets installed (in my case in a venv: venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/kerberos.cpython-35m-darwin.so). 

This .so file seems to have been compiled using clang's Address Sanitizer, and triggers this error message:
```
==78358==ERROR: Interceptors are not working. This may be because AddressSanitizer is loaded too late (e.g. via dlopen). Please launch the executable with:
DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/lib/clang/8.0.0/lib/darwin/libclang_rt.asan_osx_dynamic.dylib

    <empty stack>

Abort trap: 6
```

Now, I'm not 100% sure that it's requests-kerberos the guilty party here, but it is the only suspect. There are no more native files in my venv, and `otool -lL kerberos.cpython-35m-darwin.so` does show a smoking gun: it refers to dylib `	@rpath/libclang_rt.asan_osx_dynamic.dylib (compatibility version 0.0.0, current version 0.0.0)`, and has a load command like this:

issue_body:
the [gcloud datastore sample](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python#google-cloud-datastore) does not persist/put() the entity.   It also describes a put  followed by query (better to put followed by consistent key-get operation)

suggest updating

```
from gcloud import datastore
# Create, populate and persist an entity
entity = datastore.Entity(key=datastore.Key('EntityKind'))
entity.update({
    'foo': u'bar',
    'baz': 1337,
    'qux': False,
})
# Then query for entities
query = datastore.Query(kind='EntityKind')
for result in query.fetch():
    print result
```

perhaps  something like

```
from gcloud import datastore
ds = datastore.client.Client(dataset_id='YOUR_DATASET_ID')
# Create, populate and persist an entity
entity = datastore.Entity(key=ds.key('EntityKind'))
entity.update({
    'foo': u'bar',
    'baz': 1337,
    'qux': False,
})
ds.put(entity)
# Then get by key for this entity
result = ds.get(entity.key)
print result
```

issue_title: "Client GRPC deadline too short" for speech streaming_recognize + cannot override defaults
issue_body:
There is currently no way to override default configuration parameter values when using **google.cloud.speech.Client**. Support is available in **google.cloud.gapic.speech.v1beta1.speech_client.SpeechClient** via the **client_config** constructor argument. However, this is not used in **google.cloud.speech._gax.GAPICSpeechAPI**, where a **SpeechClient** instance is constructed without making use of **client_config**.

This means, among other things, that I cannot set the GRPC deadline myself. On the other hand, the default value for timeout present in the configuration file as **"timeout_millis"** under **"StreamingRecognize"** is **190000**, which should be perfect for a call that needs to be shorter than 1 minute anyway (required deadline is 3 * duration + 5, so 185 should already be enough). However, it looks like the actual value that is sent as a deadline comes from **"initial_rpc_timeout_millis"** field from **"retry_params"** in the configuration file (I have actually changed that value to 185000 and the error disappeared). I'm not sure why this is the case and I could not figure out where it happens in the code.

I am experiencing the issue while using the following versions of Python packages:
* gapic-google-cloud-speech-v1beta1 (0.15.3)
* google-auth (0.8.0)
* google-auth-httplib2 (0.0.2)
* google-cloud-core (0.23.1)
* google-cloud-speech (0.23.0)
* google-gax (0.15.8)
* googleapis-common-protos (1.5.2)
* grpcio (1.1.3)

issue_title: v4 - Mixin support
issue_body:
This relates to some things that were discussed prior to v4 (see #495 and #498 ) but which may be worth raising again now, since a lot of refactoring has gone on since then, primarily the switch to Sass.

As someone who currently uses the LESS version of Bootstrap (v3), I've got used to being able to use Bootstrap classes as mixins (by importing the LESS file in which a particular class is defined, then using the class in exactly the same way as a no-parameter mixin). The advantage of this is I can keep much more of the presentational layer out of the HTML and in the CSS where it really belongs.

From what I can tell, in Sass this is not possible. At least not as much. While you can use @extend to do something kind of similar, this doesn't work inside @media queries, whereas with mixins this limitation doesn't exist.

Could not more of the classes be written as mixins to make this possible? I realise there are some cases where this gets complicated, but for a lot of the simpler classes this wouldn't be a major problem would it? It would mean that for each class there would need to be a corresponding mixin (which could then be used in the class definition itself), so it would mean more lines of code, but wouldn't really increase the size of the compiled CSS.

As has been said before, this is a limitation of Sass which perhaps they need to address. But given that it exists, would it be worth taking this into consideration in Bootstrap?

issue_title: Pants 1.2.1 infrequent empty analysis files
issue_body:
I have had two reports of unusual zinc errors. In neither case did I have access to the box, and only once did I get any decent time with the engineer poking under my direction. So just some empty conjecture for now :)

    compile(src/jvm/com/foursquare/target:target_name) failed: Unrecognized version line:

interspersed with some

    compile(src/jvm/com/foursquare/flc/cohort/concrete:concrete) failed: Unexpected end-of-file parsing 

The user did have a current directory and it was a symlink. Both current and the linked unique_results_dir had analysis files, but the analysis files were empty [instead of the expected](https://github.com/pantsbuild/zincutils/blob/master/zincutils/zinc_analysis_parser.py#L144):

      format version: 5
      output mode:
      1 items
      <snip>

Like I said, this was in Pants 1.2.1, so before zinc 1.0.0. `name-hashing` is disabled, but otherwise there is nothing special involved in our zinc pipeline.

The only interesting things I noticed was that he had maybe 10 old results_dirs under there. I am not sure if we need to enable cleanup nor have I checked that code.

I also noticed that the task version and build_invalidator do not include the global cache_key, which you could imagine confusing zinc, especially if they are checking out a before/after of the recent Pants upgrade I pushed. There is test protection over changing the task version, but not the global cache_key.

But while the cache_key thing should likely be fixed, I don't know that it would explain empty analysis files. I do wonder about when the analysis gets updated from 'previous' to 'current' and whether that is atomic in any sense.

I just had them clean, which unblocked both cases.

issue_title: gpg: fatal: can't create directory `//.gnupg': Permission denied
issue_body:
I've injected the WAL-E module into a postgres docker container. I can get the streaming archiving to S3 to work when I don't incorporate encryption. However, I need to encrypt the files prior to shipping to S3. I generated a gpg key, created a WALE_GPG_KEY_ID with the key id enclosed. While attached to the container, I executed the following command: su - postgres bash -c "envdir /etc/wal-e.d/env wal-e backup-push $PGDATA"

I receive the following error:
wal-e: Could not set up syslog, continuing anyway.  Reason: ENOENT
wal_e.operator.backup INFO     MSG: start upload postgres version metadata
        DETAIL: Uploading to s3://sofi-postgresql-backups/basebackups_005/base_000000010000000000000002_00000040/extended_version.txt.

gpg: fatal: can't create directory `//.gnupg': Permission denied
secmem usage: 0/0 bytes in 0/0 blocks of pool 0/32768

Here is the log from the "docker logs <container>" command:

wal_e.worker.upload INFO     MSG: begin archiving a file

gpg: fatal: can't create directory `/root/.gnupg': Permission denied
secmem usage: 0/0 bytes in 0/0 blocks of pool 0/32768

Seems to be a permissions issue with the /root/.gnupg directory during encryption. I've altered the permissions to no avail. Any direction would be greatly appreciated.

issue_title: draft.send() does not return thread or message IDs
issue_body:
In v1.1.0, `draft.send()` does not return the thread or message IDs of the newly created thread (or existing thread, in the case where a send is a reply).

This is highly desirable for recording sends.

Current workaround is to call `draft.save()` before calling `draft.send()`, but this can create "orphaned" drafts if the `draft.send()` fails w/ a 4XX or 5XX server error, cluttering up our users' drafts folder in their email accounts.

in adwords.py the method _DownloadReportCheckFormat was recently changed. The new test for is_binary_mode is probably incorrect:

  def _DownloadReportCheckFormat(self, file_format, output):
    is_binary_mode = getattr(output, 'mode', 'w') == 'wb'
    if (file_format.startswith('GZIPPED_')

issue_title: Don't swallow AttributeErrors
issue_body:
The changes in commit https://github.com/girder/girder_worker/commit/af45e1f487ec3f5330e513a6f2c48df76796949a (and similar code throughout girder_worker) may catch far more missing attributes than just a lack of a `JobManager`. This masks real errors, preventing them from bubbling to the top of the call stack, which makes development and debugging more difficult.

Ideally, all code should use `hasattr`, `getattr`, or perform type checking of the base object. If `except AttributeError: pass` is ever used, it should not enclose code with function calls.

issue_title: flowspec: AND bit in the first operator is set
issue_body:
##### ISSUE TYPE

Bug Report

##### OS

ExaBGP is running on Ubuntu 16.04 Docker container on Ubuntu 16.04 host.

##### VERSION

```
# exabgp --version
ExaBGP : 4.0.2-1c737d99
Python : 3.5.2 (default, Nov 23 2017, 16:37:01)  [GCC 5.4.0 20160609]
Uname  : Linux 46616c3d145e 4.4.0-109-generic #132-Ubuntu SMP Tue Jan 9 19:52:39 UTC 2018 x86_64
Root   : /usr/local
```

##### ENVIRONMENT 

```
<EMPTY>
```

##### CONFIGURATION

```
neighbor 172.17.0.2 {
    router-id 192.168.0.2;
    local-address 172.17.0.3;
    local-as 65000;
    peer-as 65000;
}
```

##### SUMMARY

With the combined numeric type (maybe also bitmask type) rules like `port >=80&<=90`, ExaBGP seems to set the AND bit of in the first operator byte.
Wireshark shows `&& >=80 && <=90`;

```
...(snip)...
Border Gateway Protocol - UPDATE Message
    ...(snip)...
    Path attributes
        ...(snip)...
        Path Attribute - MP_REACH_NLRI
            ...(snip)...
            Network layer reachability information (6 bytes)
                FLOW_SPEC_NLRI (6 bytes)
                    ...(snip)...
                    Filter: Port filter (&& >=80 && <=90)
                    ...(snip)...
```

According to [RFC5575](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5575), it should be unset, then the above rule should be `>=80 && <=90` on Wireshark.

```
         a -   AND bit.  If unset, the previous term is logically ORed
               with the current one.  If set, the operation is a logical
               AND.  It should be unset in the first operator byte of a
               sequence.  The AND operator has higher priority than OR
               for the purposes of evaluating logical expressions.
```

##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE

After connection established, announce a route via "exabgpcli".

```
exabgpcli 'announce flow route { match { port >=80&<=90; } then { discard; } }'
```

issue_title: Search request data
Docker-py API don't expect getting data in list-like types (except list itself)
issue_body:
Most of the places in Docker-py have checks like `isinstance(smth, list)`, but this won't play nicely when you pass a `tuple` or a `set`.

For example, you just cannot pass anything but `list` or `str`/`unicode`/`bytes` type to `volumes` argument to `create_container()`.

issue_title: Embedding composites should also transform parameters.
issue_body:
Some parameters are "qubit specific", like initial state or anneal offsets. Would be good if they were transformed the same way the problem is transformed.

For example, suppose I have a small "logical" problem, and I use an embedding to sample from it with the hardware. If I want to use reverse annealing, the initial state I will provide will be in the "logical" space, and won't be mapped to the "embedded" space before being passed to the child sampler.

issue_title: [BioformatsReader] timestamps assignment needs revision
issue_body:
This issue applies to files generated on a Zeiss microscope using their software (Zen Black in this case);
I have not had the opportunity to check other sources.

When the dataset is a time sequence of z-stacks, `pims` associates the timestamps  with the z-slices rather than with the frames (here a frame refers to a single z-stack in the time sequence).
When reading the timestamps off the `t_s` entry in the metadata, they show `None`s starting at the slice which is at the `T` position, where `T` is the size of the `t`-axis.

To reproduce the issue:

```python
## read a CZI file which is a time sequence of z-stacks
czifile = 'path/to/file'
images = pims.bioformats.BioformatsReader(czifile)

## inspect the timestamps where the issue shows up
last_frame = images.sizes['t'] // images.sizes['z']
last_slice = images.sizes['t']%images.sizes['z']
print('last time stamp expected at frame #{}, slice #{} : {}\n and is followed by a **{}**'.format(last_frame, last_slice, images[last_frame].metadata['t_s'][last_slice-1],images[last_frame].metadata['t_s'][last_slice]))
```

As temporary solution, one can read the timestamps using the (excellant!) API `pims` provides:

`frames_ts = [images.metadata.PlaneDeltaT(0, n) for n in range(images.sizes['t'])]`

PS. a quick note to the Devs: 
I find `pims` very intuitive and useful; Thank you so much for your work!

issue_title: Refactor code for minimal RSS size & startup time (especially for "service master" mode)
store: Re-template ceph.conf after daemon/monitor are started
issue_body:
In the bin/boot scripts for [store-daemon](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/e99ae10dd9e7fa767a9a7b48d76a33ad07534555/store/daemon/bin/boot#L54), [store-metadata](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/e99ae10dd9e7fa767a9a7b48d76a33ad07534555/store/metadata/bin/boot#L64), [store-monitor](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/e99ae10dd9e7fa767a9a7b48d76a33ad07534555/store/monitor/bin/boot#L88), we template `/etc/ceph/ceph.conf` with confd, and then exec the Ceph process. Unfortunately, this means that for long-running store components, we won't pick up changes to monitors until the container is restarted.

I think that the Ceph process will be happy as it only reads the configuration to get a list of monitors to query for state, but for anyone running `ceph -s` within the container, they'll see a lot of faults. 

I'm hoping this can be solved as part of #2701 when we rewrite the bin/boot scripts in Go, as we'll need to run both the daemon process and confd.

issue_title: Proposal: deis cli discrete git remote command
issue_body:
Currently `$ deis apps:create foo` (optionally) adds a git remote.

If an app is accessed by multiple people then the others have to create their remote manually.

Heroku toolbelt has a command for this:

```
$ heroku help git
Additional commands, type "heroku help COMMAND" for more details:

  git:clone APP [DIRECTORY]  #  clones a heroku app to your local machine at DIRECTORY (defaults to app name)
  git:remote [OPTIONS]       #  adds a git remote to an app repo
```

I propose adding a `deis git:remote --app foo --remote deis` command to the deis CLI.

issue_title: Allow NaN or None values to be passed in, and silently ignored
issue_body:
In my DataFrames, I oftentimes find it very reasonable to have quite a few NaN or None values. 

In order to run tsfresh, I just set all those values to 0, which is... not ideal. Even imputing missing values would not work particularly well for several of my use cases. 

Yet, it seems (from a super naive outsider's perspective), like this is filtering that tsfresh could do relatively easily itself. 

When it grabs each time_series, it can simply remove or categorically ignore NaN/None, and compute features on the values that do exist. This makes my life easier when, say, one customer signs up a month after another customer, and thus has missing values for that month. 

Again, super naive outsider perspective here, I know this might be impossible. But if it is possible, I'd love to add in that bit of filtering!

issue_title: Add PEP 484 type hinting to Populus
issue_body:
Proposal/suggestion: add PEP 484 type hinting to Populus.

https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0484/ specifies optional type hinting, which is supported by IDEs like PyCharm, and static checkers like `mypy`.

I can just maintain stub files in my own workspace, but it seems like it might be useful to add these to the project.

There are a couple ways this can be done, the simplest is just adding a `.pyi` file for every `.py` file. I created a WIP here https://github.com/ethereum/populus/pull/441 to explore what this would look like.

Another possibility would be to keep the type hints out-of-tree, perhaps in https://github.com/python/typeshed or some other repo. That seems easier to lose in a refactor.

Any thoughts/preferences on how to proceed here?

issue_title: Add support for `bytearray`s in the various conversion utils
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

We don't support `bytearrays` in most/all of the various conversion utils.  Since `bytearray` and `bytes` types are interchangeable in *many* ways, and supporting both is trivial, we should add support.

### How can it be fixed?

Add support to the conversion utils for `bytearray` types in the primitive field, like: `to_int`, `to_bytes`, `to_hex`, `to_text` and anywhere else that supports a `bytes` type as an input.

issue_title: CONDA_CHANNELS env variable is not consistent for different Minicondas
issue_body:
The https://github.com/astropy/ci-helpers repo provides a toolkit for a large number of packages to set up the conda environment for their travis and appveyor runs. We have a few environment variables including ``CONDA_CHANNELS`` that we process in our install scripts.

Until yesterday we downloaded the ``Miniconda-latest*`` version of Miniconda to start the install, but due to #4562, I had to change it to be ``Miniconda3-*`` (or ``Miniconda2-*``).

Now I'm running into the very annoying bug that this variable also seems to be a conda variable (though we haven't noticed it to be documented at the time we introduced it, nor can I find it documented now).
 
We use whitespace as separator when multiple channels are given, while you seem to prefer a coma separated string. This discrepancy make the install fail quite badly with a ``CondaHTTPError: The remote server could not find the channel you requested.``

There is also a discrepancy between the behaviour of the install scripts in this matter, as both Miniconda2- and Miniconda3- knows about this env variable, while Miniconda- doesn't.

I wonder whether it is possible to choose whitespace as a separator, and if not then make it a separator, too. 

Failure with Miniconda3-: https://travis-ci.org/astropy/ci-helpers/jobs/199371961
Failure with Miniconda2-: https://travis-ci.org/astropy/ci-helpers/jobs/199439273
All green with Miniconda-: https://travis-ci.org/astropy/ci-helpers/jobs/199575722

issue_title: Need new cmdlet for NetworkWatcherNetworkConfigurationDiagnostic API
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
We need a cmdlet for the new API. NetworkConfigurationDiagnostic API allows customer to get an information on if traffic is allowed or denied for configured filters. Each filter consists of direction, protocol, source, destination and sourcePort and destinationPort.

Here is rest API spec: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/master/specification/network/resource-manager/Microsoft.Network/stable/2018-06-01/networkWatcher.json (line 1781)
Example of usage: https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/master/specification/network/resource-manager/Microsoft.Network/stable/2018-06-01/examples/NetworkWatcherNetworkConfigurationDiagnostic.json

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Our suggestion is to create 2 cmdlet. One is for creating traffic query. Another is for getting network configuration diagnostic.

az network watcher traffic query create --direction --protocol --source --destination --destinationPort
az network watcher network configuration diagnostic --location --targetResourceId --traficeQueries<List<trafficQuery>>

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

Constructing a BlockBlobService with a Management URL
issue_body:
This is mostly an issue about (my) user error.  I am trying to understand the best way to construct a BlockBlobService with a management URL.

I construct a ServiceManagementService client like so.

```python
management_url = "https://management.core.windows.net"

sms = ServiceManagementService(subscription_id='--subscription-id--', cert_file='--cert-file--', host=management_url)
```

I want to construct a BlockBlobService, but based on the constructor arguments I cannot find a good way to just pass in the management_url.  There are parameters for custom domain and endpoint_suffix. Ideally, I just want to pass in the mangement_url.

Instead, I work around it by doing this, which seems wrong.

```python
client = BlockBlobService('--name--', '--primary-key--')
client.primary_endpoint = management_url
```

Is there a better way to implement this?

issue_title: aiohttp 2.x closing client request data stream
issue_body:
In previous versions of aiohttp, if you passed a stream as the `data` parameter to an aiohttp request, it would not close the stream after the request, now it is closing it because it's wrapping it in a `Payload`, and later the `write` method closes it.

I believe aiohttp should not be closing the stream sent to it. This new behavior caused this issue with aiobotocore: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiobotocore/issues/221 resulting in retries failing.

issue_title: How can I set the event loop's task factory using the pytest plugin?
issue_body:
## Long story short

I have introduced `aiotask_context` to an existing project in order to implement something like a request context in aiohttp using the middleware feature, which then enabled me to scope SQLAlchemy sessions to the request by using the `scopedsession` factory class. However, doing so made all the aiohttp pytest tests to fail, which was expected but I don't know how to fix them properly. The application itself runs without any errors when tested manually, the tests however are failing.

## Expected behaviour

All tests passing using the client fixture:

```python
import pytest

from example.app import create_app


@pytest.fixture
async def client(aiohttp_client):
    app = create_app()
    return await aiohttp_client(app)
```

Example test:

```python
import pytest
from tests.common import PING_API_URL


@pytest.mark.usefixtures("client")
class TestGet:
    async def test_get_healthy(self, client):
        response = await client.get(PING_API_URL)
        assert response.status == 200
        text = await response.text()
        assert text == '"pong"'
```

## Actual behaviour

This is the error I get when run the tests:

```
  File "/path/to/Workspace/example-app/example/middlewares.py", line 89, in middleware
    context.set("X-Request-ID", request.headers.get("X-Request-ID", str(uuid.uuid4())))
  File "path/to/Workspace/example-app/.testing-venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiotask_context/__init__.py", line 115, in set
    asyncio.Task.current_task().context[key] = value
AttributeError: '_asyncio.Task' object has no attribute 'context'
```

## Steps to reproduce

Implement the following middleware and database sessions scoping function for your SQLAlchemy Session factory class:

```python
import asyncio
import aiotask_context as context
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, scoped_session


###
# Middleware
#
async def request_id_middleware(app, handler):
    async def middleware_handler(request):
        context.set("X-Request-ID", request.headers.get("X-Request-ID", str(uuid.uuid4())))
        response = await handler(request)
        response.headers["X-Request-ID"] = context.get("X-Request-ID")
        return response
    return middleware_handler


###
# Scoping function
#
def get_current_request() -> Optional[str]:
    return context.get("X-Request-ID", None)


###
# Application
#
_app = web.Application(
    logger=_logger,
    middlewares=(
        request_id_middleware,
    ),
)


###
# Database w/ SQLAlchemy
#
_db_engine = _create_database_engine()
Session = scoped_session(
    sessionmaker(bind=_db_engine),
    scopefunc=get_current_request)


###
# Running the application
#
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop.set_task_factory(context.task_factory)

app = create_app()

web.run_app(app=app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8080)
```

## Your environment

 * Ubuntu 16.04
 * Pyenv
 * Python v3.6.3
 * Aiohttp v3.1.0
 * aiotask_context v0.5.0
 * SQLAlchemy v1.2.5

galaxy_config.py sometimes checking install from boolean sometimes from kwds
issue_body:
I had a bug where galaxy_config was unable to write the shed_tool_conf_contents
https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/blob/master/planemo/galaxy_config.py#L235-L236
because the parent directory did not exist.

(although this was later hard to replicate)

I think this is because sometimes install_galaxy is a boolean as set in.
https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/blob/master/planemo/galaxy_config.py#L110

But when it actually decides if it should install galaxy in _install_galaxy_if_needed
https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/blob/master/planemo/galaxy_config.py#L481-L489
The code looks only at kwds which have not been updated to the boolean install_galaxy

One possible fix is to record install_galaxy to kwds just below 
https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/blob/master/planemo/galaxy_config.py#L110

When I later tried to replicate this I received the completely correct.
FAILED_TO_FIND_GALAXY_EXCEPTION
Which is probably why this has not happened often.

issue_title: add a warning for modules with duplicate names
issue_body:
I would like to import multiple modules to a common import directory and have each one stored individually in a folder named with its repository name.

A similar scenario is like when you untar ```foo.tar``` and it saves its contents to ```foo/```, and when untarring multiple tarballs in a common directory you get a folder for each untarred archive.

I did a simple test with this config below and it resulted in a nested import dir, called ```*```, which I found odd, because ```mkdir *``` fails.

```
imports:
  plugin: plugins/*

git module plugin:
  url: https://github.com/mitchtbaum/pelican_reference
```

With this config, instead of merging both modules (intelligently or even with collisions), only the last item specified for that output location will show up:

```
imports:
  plugin: plugins/

git module plugin:
  url: https://github.com/rdegges/pelican-minify

git module plugin:
  url: https://github.com/mitchtbaum/pelican_reference
```

issue_title: Dependency in __init__ means pip install fails
issue_body:
pyparsing is a dependency for bibtexparser and is noted as such in setup.py.

**However**, setup.py gets the version number from __init__, which in turn imports bibtexexpression, which imports pyparsing.

As a result, in an install where pyparsing is not already installed, attempting to install via pip will result in an error:

    $ cd /path/to/project
    $ virtualenv .
    $ source ./bin/activate
    $ pip install -e git+https://github.com/sciunto-org/python-bibtexparser.git#egg=bibtexparser
    Obtaining bibtexparser from git+https://github.com/sciunto-org/python-bibtexparser.git#egg=bibtexparser
      Updating ./src/bibtexparser clone
        Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
        Traceback (most recent call last):
          File "<string>", line 20, in <module>
          File "/path/to/project/src/bibtexparser/setup.py", line 8, in <module>
            from bibtexparser import __version__ as version
          File "bibtexparser/__init__.py", line 27, in <module>
            from . import bibdatabase, bibtexexpression, bparser, bwriter, latexenc, customization
          File "bibtexparser/bibtexexpression.py", line 1, in <module>
            import pyparsing as pp
        ImportError: No module named pyparsing
    
        ----------------------------------------
    Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /path/to/project/src/bibtexparser

issue_title: controller(pysc2/lib/remote_controller.py) observed actions  missing target minimap information
Make the 'temp' Broker support monitoring
issue_body:
Because sqlite does not support threading, it's not possible to use the sqlite-backed header source (which is the one we use for `Broker.named('temp')`) with any plans that involve monitoring. The RunEngine emits documents on a background thread, and it's dispatcher tries to pass them to `Broker.insert` on that same background thread, which silently fails and just drops the data on the floor.

I _think_ this could be fixed in the sqlite header source. When documents are inserted, they can be cached in a queue managed by us and drained into sqlite some time later. The goal is to ensure that interaction with sqlite only happens on a single thread. (Does it have to be the main thread? Not sure.) I have in mind something akin to matplotlib's `draw_idle()` method, which addresses a similar issue -- that issue being that Qt can only draw on the main thread.

issue_title: Complete DocStrings
issue_body:
Review modules to ensure every module, function and method is documented with a complete docstring.

_For Package & Modules - DocStrings should..._
* Enumerate the package's or modules's exposed API; providing simple 1-2 sentence descriptions.
* Call out any notable requirements or key pieces of information when using the package / module.

_For Classes, Functions & Methods - DocStrings should..._
* Provide a simple 1-sentence description describing purpose / use.
* Provide a more complete description of use if needed.
* Enumerate attributes, arguments, return types, and exceptions raised.

_All DocStrings_ should use the **Imperative Tense or Mood**, and should use **package consistent formatting** (intent is to use an auto-generation tool to assist with package documentation).

issue_title: Make sure that arguments that can take None as a value have a default value

issue_title: Are jws and letsencrypt-renewer meant to be used by end-users?
issue_body:
There are currently three binaries installed system-wide in `/usr/bin`:

- `letsencrypt`
- `jws`
- `letsencrypt-renewer`

The first one is obviously what end-users will be using, but what about the other two? Are they internal binaries used by other tools / cron jobs?

If so, then in Debian, they will need to live somewhere like `/usr/lib/letsencrypt/` or `/usr/share/letsencrypt`. Do we need extra config somewhere to let the tools find these binaries or should I run the right files through `sed` to fix the paths?

issue_title: Build error on OS X 10.9.5
issue_body:
```
$ python setup.py build
...
building '_vmprof' extension
/usr/bin/clang -Wno-unused-result -Wsign-compare -Wunreachable-code -fno-common -dynamic -DNDEBUG -g -fwrapv -O3 -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -pipe -Os -I/opt/local/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/include/python3.5m -c src/_vmprof.c -o build/temp.macosx-10.9-x86_64-3.5/src/_vmprof.o -Wno-unused
In file included from src/_vmprof.c:31:
In file included from src/vmprof_main.h:34:
src/vmprof_mt.h:69:46: error: use of undeclared identifier 'MAP_ANONYMOUS'
                               MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS,
```

I'm not sure how you want to handle this (`ifdef`, platform check, or just switching to `MAP_ANON`), but the following works on OS X 10.9.5.

```diff
diff --git a/src/vmprof_mt.h b/src/vmprof_mt.h
index bff40d4..7090102 100644
--- a/src/vmprof_mt.h
+++ b/src/vmprof_mt.h
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@ static int prepare_concurrent_bufs(void)
     unprepare_concurrent_bufs();
     profbuf_all_buffers = mmap(NULL, sizeof(struct profbuf_s) * MAX_NUM_BUFFERS,
                                PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE,
-                               MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS,
+                               MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANON,
                                -1, 0);
     if (profbuf_all_buffers == MAP_FAILED) {
         profbuf_all_buffers = NULL;
```

[This](http://reviews.llvm.org/D15180) mentions that `MAP_ANONYMOUS` is only available on OS X 10.11 and later.

issue_title: Feedback on error message
issue_body:
Hello. I had a strange bug that caused me a lot of frustration. This may be solved by the new plugin work you've been doing but here is my setup:

1. apispec - v0.38.0 (I'm doing something weird and haven't fixed it to update yet)
2. Using tornado

I was intermittently failing when calling `add_path`. The error I was getting was:

```
APISpecError('Path template is not specified')
```

The core problem was, I had a method definition as the following:

```
@coroutine
def post(self, *args, **kwargs):
    pass
```

The `*args` and `**kwargs` should never have been there but got populated by my editor automatically. At any rate, this came down to line 95 in `ext/tornado.py`. The type error that was generated was swallowed during the `add_path` call masking the problem a bit.

Even more curious is the fact that the failure was not consistent. It might be something wonky with my environment, but at some point, the following line was returning different output:

```
args = list(inspect.signature(method).parameters.keys())[1:]
```

Sometimes the output of this function would be `['args', 'kwargs']` and other times it would be `[]`. I did some initial testing to try to replicate the problem in a stripped down environment, but could never reproduce. 

This turned out to be a little longer than I expected. Honestly, I'm not sure what I would expect you to do about it, except maybe point me more in the right direction with a more helpful error message?

Thanks so much for the library! It makes my life much easier, and I appreciate all the hard work. Let me know if I can help fix this problem.

issue_title: sklearn.convert supports GradientBoostingRegressor only with default value of loss function (loss=‘ls’)

But GradientBoostingRegressor(..., loss=‘ls’) will be ok for converting. As I understand 'ls' does not use QuantileEstimator class (in scikit-learn). Thats why converter works. But in https://apple.github.io/coremltools/generated/coremltools.converters.sklearn.convert.html  there are no any comments about this limitation. Gradient Boosting Classifier and Regressor marked as supported.

issue_title: `obj_update` will take one more database search when bundle.obj.pk is an int value

These code lines make `obj_update` function always raise NotFound when bundle.obj is not None:

        bundle_detail_data = self.get_bundle_detail_data(bundle) if bundle.obj else None
        arg_detail_data = kwargs.get(self._meta.detail_uri_name, None)

        if not bundle_detail_data or (arg_detail_data and bundle_detail_data != arg_detail_data):

The reason is `get_bundle_detail_data(bundle)` always return an int num and `kwargs.get(self._meta.detail_uri_name, None)` always return a str num, their returns must not be equal.

issue_title: Precision@K values higher than 1
issue_body:
My understanding is that P@K values above 1 should not be possible, but I've managed to find a way. I'm fairly certain there are some constraints I'm missing in my problem setup, and looks like a good opportunity to update the docs and/or include some new error handling in the code (both of which I'm happy to help out with if I can).

Here's the basic setup, and I'll be happy to dig into details as much as necessary...
- I'm attempting to create a user matching recommender for a dating site. There are 6550 users and I'm treating users as items as well as users.
- I'm encoding right swipes as 1's and everything else as 0's (left or unswiped)
- I randomly assign 20% of all swipes to the test data. Not taking most recent, or ensuring that all users are represented in both train and test data
- I'm using the WARP loss function
- I have ~50 user features that I've extracted from their profile, but for testing purposes I've reduced the feature set down to: age, weight, and height. Weight and height have null values. None of these features have been normalized (is that necessary?)

When I fit the model without features (30 epochs, learning rate: .05) I get:
mean AUC: train 0.86, test 0.87   *** this is kinda odd too, test is always equal or higher
mean P@k: train 0.48, test 0.43

This seems to be pretty good, and when I examine the ranked recommendations they are appropriately all different for each user

If I add my user features (which I've tried both by adding as both user_features and item_features, and also just as user_features), I get:
mean AUC: train 0.88, test 0.89
mean P@k: train 11.41, test 2.96

Seems impossible. And also - every user's top 5 recommendations are out of the same set of 6-7 "items". There's no variation.

Does anything stick out as being obviously wrong conceptually?

issue_title: Dataframe input for functions
issue_body:
From a conversation started in #37 by @bmu :
>As an idea, they could possibly also be used by users in the future, if we would switch to dataframe inputs for some functions or a different kind of api. This would enable us to include som e "magic" functions, e.g the signature of the ominous globalinplane function could look like this:

    def globalinplane(df, surface_tilt, surface_azimuth, diffuse_model='perez', 
                  decomposition_model=None):
    """Determine GPOA from either GHI and DHI or from GHI only

    Parameters
    ---------------
    df : pandas.DataFrame
          A DataFrame containing all necessary columns acoording to naming conventions
          (maybe surface_tilt and surface_azimuth could also be contained in the df, 
           e.g. for tracking systems.) 
    decomposition_model : None or str
          The model to use if only GHI is given in the DataFrame
    ....
    Returns
    -------
    The input DataFrame plus columns `direct tilted`, 'diffuse tilted`, `GPOA` ...
    """"
>This function could look which columns are in the DataFrame and compute all necessary columns (e.g. if time is given as local time, compute utc or true solar time).

>From my experience this is usefull for beginners because there are quite a lot of simulation steps required to calculate GPOA from GHI (maybe convert times, decomposition in direct and diffuse, diffuse model, ground reflection, direct tilted, ...?).
And there are other options, e.g. claculate expected energy yield from only a system description, Location and a DataFrame containing GHI and ambient temperature.

>This is just an idea, not sure about the difficulties. There may be some complexity when implementing something like this (more on the definitions, not necessarily from a programmers point of view) and maybe this is difficult to explain to users.

My veiw on this would be to stay away from datframe passing as inputs, especially as the only form of input. One of the advantages of pvlib is that there is the ability to use  different inputs (irradiance sources, plane transposition models, etc.) and compare their outputs from the functions. This means that a user might have multiple versions of dni,ghi, pmp, etc. which they are wanting to use in the functions. Though it is possible to repackage a new dataframe for each time a variable is swapped out, this leads to extra unnecessary steps on the user side, and makes it harder to explicitly track what is being passed through a function. When I was originally making tools for myself, I did make them with dataframe inputs, and found that it was leading to too many hard to trace errors, and ended up switching it to explicit inputs.  

It might be interesting to have df input as an optional input along with the explicit variables (which might have been what you were suggesting), but I wouldn't want to move completely to df input.

issue_title: APIC Tag Uses Image Description
issue_body:
Hello, I just wanted to say, it seems to me the way APIC keys are handled in mutagen 1.36.2 is not a very good approach. Creating keys of the form `APIC:<image-description>` does not seem like a good idea since there is no guarantee that a user's music library has standardized image descriptions. Couldn't we use the fact we know if an image is a front cover to create a key like `APIC:Cover_Front` or whatever type the image is?

This comes up because I am writing some scripts to deal with my music library, and some of my files have front covers with descriptions saying "Front Cover" and others are the name of the file that was added as the front cover. I would like to be able to just say I want all the front covers. And this should be possible since that information is included in APIC tags, correct?

#connection_string = args.connect
connection_string = "/dev/ttyAMA0,57600"
print("Connecting to...% s" % connection_string)
vehicle = connect(connection_string, wait_ready=True)

#Function to arm and takeoff to a specified altitude
print(vehicle.mode)

# vehicle.mode = VehicleMode("LAND")
# time.sleep(5)
def arm_and_takeoff(aTargetAlt):
	print("Basic Prearm checks..dont touch!!")

 	# while not vehicle.is_armable:
		# print("Waiting for vehicle to initialize")
		# time.sleep(2)

	print("Arming Motors..")
	# Copter should arm in Guided-mode
	vehicle.mode = VehicleMode("GUIDED")
	time.sleep(3)
	vehicle.armed = True
	
	while not vehicle.armed:
		print("Waiting for arming..:% s"% vehicle.mode)
		vehicle.armed = True
		time.sleep(2)
		
	print("Taking Off..")
	vehicle.simple_takeoff(aTargetAlt)
	time.sleep(5)

	while True:
		print("Altitude: ",vehicle.location.global_relative_frame.alt)
		#Break and return from function just below target altitude.
		if vehicle.location.global_relative_frame.alt>=aTargetAlt*0.95: 
			print("Reached Target Altitude..")
			modeLand()
			# print("Landing....")
			# vehicle.mode = VehicleMode("LAND");
			# print("Vehicle mode:%s"% vehicle.mode)
			break


def modeLand():
	print("Landing now")
	vehicle.mode = VehicleMode("LAND")
	time.sleep(5)
	print("-------------Vehicle is in:-------%s"% vehicle.mode)

arm_and_takeoff(2)
print(vehicle.mode)
print("Vehicle object closed")
vehicle.close()
```
  
And I have Optical flow Camera and a Lidar which i can use.
Please help I'm stuck with this.

Regards

issue_title: Have protection (or offer support) for 'odd' characters in Job name (e.g. '<..>')
issue_body: There is very little (if any) protection for what you can set a Job name to in Ganga. We should either disallow the known broken things (XML tags for example) or improve the code to support them.

issue_title: GaussSplitter not overriding LHCbApp for GaudiExec
issue_body:
I paste the email below. We had a problem before with the GaussSplitter overriding the LHCbApp options but it seems here to still not interacting with them properly.

I have 2 different jobs with 2 subjobs each. The former uses a private version of Gauss with GaudiExec, the latter a default Gauss version. Both use GaussSplitter

 GaussSplitter (
   numberOfJobs = 2,
   eventsPerJob = 50,
   firstEventNumber = 0
 )

as splitter. In the option file I setup the number of events to generate with

LHCbApp().EvtMax = 100

If I run the default version of Gauss then I get 50 events per subjobs correctly uncorrelated --> Don't know why the NextNext event (spillover event) seems to have the same seeds between subjobs, but the main event have different seeds so it is fine
If I run the same option file with GaudiExec then I get 100 events per subjobs, 50 of each are totally correlated among the 2 subjobs since they have the same seeds.
I know I can easily avoid the problem if I change

LHCbApp().EvtMax = 100 --> LHCbApp().EvtMax = 50

when I run with GaudiExec, but I'm wondering if this is a bug or a known behaviour.

issue_title: Regression/Bug when filtering with integer values in PostgreSQL storage
issue_body:
The bug was probably introduced in #761 

Reproduce steps:

* Run Kinto with PostgreSQL storage
* Create a record `{"age": 12}`
* Create a record `{"name": "captain hook"}`
* Fetch the list with `records?min_age=10`

```
BackendError: DataError: (psycopg2.DataError) invalid input syntax for type numeric: ""
 [SQL: "
         WITH total_filtered AS (
             SELECT COUNT(id) AS count
               FROM records
              WHERE parent_id = %(parent_id)s
                AND collection_id = %(collection_id)s
                AND coalesce(data->>%(filters_field_0_0)s, '')::numeric >= %(filters_value_0)s
         ),
         collection_filtered AS (
             SELECT id, last_modified, data
               FROM records
              WHERE parent_id = %(parent_id)s
                AND collection_id = %(collection_id)s
                AND coalesce(data->>%(filters_field_0_0)s, '')::numeric >= %(filters_value_0)s
              LIMIT 10000
         ),
         fake_deleted AS (
             SELECT (%(deleted_field)s)::JSONB AS data
         ),
         filtered_deleted AS (
             SELECT id, last_modified, fake_deleted.data AS data
               FROM deleted, fake_deleted
              WHERE parent_id = %(parent_id)s
                AND collection_id = %(collection_id)s
                AND coalesce(data->>%(filters_field_0_0)s, '')::numeric >= %(filters_value_0)s
                LIMIT 0
         ),
         all_records AS (
             SELECT * FROM filtered_deleted
              UNION ALL
             SELECT * FROM collection_filtered
         ),
         paginated_records AS (
             SELECT DISTINCT id
               FROM all_records
               
         )
         SELECT total_filtered.count AS count_total,
                a.id, as_epoch(a.last_modified) AS last_modified, a.data
           FROM paginated_records AS p JOIN all_records AS a ON (a.id = p.id),
                total_filtered
           ORDER BY last_modified DESC
           ;
         "] [parameters: {'filters_field_0_0': u'readposition', 'collection_id': 'record', 'deleted_field': '{"deleted":true}', 'filters_value_0': '100', 'parent_id': u'/buckets/1301df10-6ba4-a783-7ca4-f66851a1ebf5/collections/tata'}] lang=None uid=05fb445063da0e1a188d1e8f03009ae08f461d24ce08c6880b2ef569bdb6a2ed
```

What happens is that the `coalesce(data->>%(filters_field_0_0)s, '')::numeric` is resolved to `''::numeric` which fails.

issue_title: Repeatedly changing exposure of d435 brings down a camera
issue_body:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D435 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 5.9.9.2 | 
| Operating System & Version |  Linux (Ubuntu 16) | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  tegra ubuntu 4.4.38                                        | 
| Platform                                 | NVIDIA Jetson  |
| SDK Version                            |  2.9.1 }                          | 


### Issue Description
We are running a system with two d435 cameras connected to an NVIDIA TX2. We discovered that repeatedly changing exposure on one camera will bring the second camera down.

We used the following bash script to invoke ros param server to change the exposure settings:

`#!/bin/bash`
`realsense2_camera="/sensors/camera_front_forward/realsense2_camera"`
`while :`
`do`
`#loop infinitely`
`rosrun dynamic_reconfigure dynparam set $realsense2_camera rs435_depth_exposure 200`
`sleep 5`
`rosrun dynamic_reconfigure dynparam set $realsense2_camera rs435_depth_exposure 30`
`sleep 5`
`done`

With the latest firmware released by Intel we can get this script to bring down a camera after about 5 hours run time ( interestingly it brings down the second camera down, and not the one on which we change the exposure rates)

With a previous version of firmware we can reproduce this in about two hours with the script above.
  

We have applied the UVC Linux drivers to the NVIDIA Tegra kernel patch as per installation instructions.

issue_title: SR300 reports incorrect values for Auto Range Thresholds
issue_body:
| Required Info  |   |
|---|---|
| Camera Model | SR300 | 
| Firmware Version | 3.10.10.0  | 
| Operating System & Version | Ubuntu 16.04.1 LTS  |
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)  | 4.4.0-31-generic |
| Build System |  Makefile | 

After calling `rs_set_device_option` for RS_OPTION_SR300_AUTO_RANGE_UPPER_THRESHOLD or RS_OPTION_SR300_AUTO_RANGE_LOWER_THRESHOLD for a value greater than 32k,
Calling `rs_get_device_option` for the save option returns negative values.

For example, setting RS_OPTION_SR300_AUTO_RANGE_UPPER_THRESHOLD to 40914, returns the value of -24622.

The Binary value for 40914:
`1001111111010010`
The Binary value for -24622:
`1001111111010010`
You can see that it is the same value, but one is treated as signed and the other as signed.

The data type for storing the value is an 16-bit unsigned integer which can only store between −32,768 to 32,767. The valid range for those settings is 0 to 65,535 which needs either a 32-bit integer or an unsigned 16-bit integer.

issue_title: Some questions on HW synchronization
issue_body:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       |  D415 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 5.9.14 | 
| Operating System & Version |   Win 10 | 
| Platform                                 | PC.  |
| SDK Version                            | 2.14.0                          | 
| Language                            |  C++                         | 

### Issue Description
For the days I've been trying to use HW sync to (of course) synchronize 2 D415 devices, either remotely or on the same machine and my results have not been exactly robust. So, some questions have arose on how to setup properly a HW synchronized system, beyond the suggestions of this [whitepaper](https://realsense.intel.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/63/Multiple_Camera_WhitePaper_rev1.1.pdf)


- **RC filter**
How crucial is it to have filters at the input of the sensors. When do ESD events happen and what do they depend on?

- **Frame Drop**
Suppose a system of two devices, a master and a slave.
  - What **should** happen when master drops a frame.
  - When master drops a frame, does he produce signal at his output pins?If not, will slave device still acquire frame?
  - Can the slave device fail to acquire a frame despite the fact master did acquire frame?


- **C++ API frame acquizition**
What is the _preferred_ way of acquiring frames in a HW synced system (wait4frames,poll4frames,async_syncer,syncer)? Does it even matter?


-  **Inter Camera Synchronization Setting**
   - As I have observed ,after having set the sensors to be master/slave 
`depth_sensor1.set_option(RS2_OPTION_INTER_CAM_SYNC_MODE, 1);`
`depth_sensor2.set_option(RS2_OPTION_INTER_CAM_SYNC_MODE, 2);`
a restart of the devices (running application) is necessary. Does this observation holds?Is it something anyone else has observed?
   - In the case that a restart is needed (as previously stated), sometimes devices fail to acquire and frames and a unplug/plug is needed. Again, anyone has observed this behavior? Is there a better way overcoming this problem? Unplugging/re-plugging resets the setting master/slave, so this leads to question 1 of this section :P .

- **Exposure Duration**
I've seen somewhere in an issue that in order to have HW sync, one must disable Auto Exposure and set it manual at some levels that I fail to find. What are those values? Do I have to disable AE in both sensors (color - depth)? Let's say that depth AE must be set to **X**, does in have to be exactly this value or I can go a little higher or lower?How does it affect the HW sync functionality? 


Thanks in advace, Vlad.

issue_title: need_task_ids should default to False
issue_body: We currently default to True for backward compatibility reasons, but I would venture to guess that vast majority of users do not care about the IDs of the tasks that their emitted tuples went to.  Disabling is a nice free speedup, but if we made it disabled by default, we would be cutting out extra work for most users.

issue_title: Option for strict key ordering in results.
issue_body:
Related to #18, sometimes users might want their results to be in the same order as they are in the fields. In this case, I am thinking that it should be an optional argument (default False) to the `Executor`.

In the default, it will use regular `dict` objects to store results. But if say `enforce_strict_ordering=True` is passed to the Executor, then we will use `OrderedDict` instead.

issue_title: Bidirectional relationships require a specific load order
Separating django-filter from relay connection
issue_body:
### Problem
Right now, the use of django-filter is tightly coupled with the use of the relay graphql format. I think it would be useful to separate the two so that filtering can be applied to a normal graphql list as well.

### Solution
Pull out the django-filter logic from [DjangoFilterConnectionField](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/filter/fields.py#L10) into something like `DjangoFilterMixin`. Then apply that mixin to `DjangoFilterConnectionField` and a new `DjangoFilterField`.

I've done something like this with success already. There's a lot of copy-pasta though, and I think others could benefit from it. Here's what we are currently using.
```python
from graphene import Field

class DjangoFilterField(Field):

    def __init__(self, _type, fields=None, extra_filter_meta=None,
                 filterset_class=None, *args, **kwargs):

        _fields = _type._meta.filter_fields
        _model = _type._meta.model

        self.fields = fields or _fields
        meta = dict(model=_model, fields=self.fields)
        if extra_filter_meta:
            meta.update(extra_filter_meta)
        self.filterset_class = get_filterset_class(filterset_class, **meta)
        self.filtering_args = get_filtering_args_from_filterset(self.filterset_class, _type)
        kwargs.setdefault('args', {})
        kwargs['args'].update(self.filtering_args)
        super().__init__(List(_type), *args, **kwargs)

    @staticmethod
    def list_resolver(manager, filterset_class, filtering_args,
                      root, args, context, info):
        filter_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in args.items() if k in filtering_args}
        qs = manager.get_queryset()
        qs = filterset_class(data=filter_kwargs, queryset=qs).qs
        return qs

    def get_resolver(self, parent_resolver):
        return partial(self.list_resolver, self.type._meta.model._default_manager,
                       self.filterset_class, self.filtering_args)
```

@syrusakbary 
If this sounds like a good idea, I'd be happy to whip up the PR.

issue_title: Visdom & Pillow-simd
issue_body:
I am using pillow-simd, which is a drop-in replacement for pillow that offers a speedup for certain operations by using SIMD operations of your CPU.  
However, when installing visdom, it installs pillow, which overwrites my pillow-simd install.

They solved this in pytorch/vision, by checking if pillow-simd is installed on the system in __setup.py__.
```python
from pkg_resources import get_distribution, DistributionNotFound

def get_dist(pkgname):
    try:
        return get_distribution(pkgname)
    except DistributionNotFound:
        return None

requirements = [ ... ]
pillow_ver = ' >= 4.1.1'
pillow_req = 'pillow-simd' if get_dist('pillow-simd') is not None else 'pillow'
requirements.append(pillow_req + pillow_ver)
```

I think the only drawback of using this, is if you provide wheels, as it would then be unable to determine whether you use pillow-simd or not.  
I guess this is no concern here right?

issue_title: Explore issue with automated transcript generation
issue_body:
Currently the ability to automatically generate a transcript with **history -t transcript_file.txt** works well, but not perfectly.  There are a minority of cases where it generates a transcript which nominally looks correct but which fails actual transcript testing.

The automated transcript generation feature is awesome if users can count on it 100% of the time (or at least very close to it).  So we need to figure out what is going wrong in the minority of cases.  Perhaps we need to do something slightly different when generating the transcript.  Or perhaps, transcript testing could be a little bit more permissive, particularly towards differences in trailing whitespace at the end of a line, end of a command, and end of the file.

The one case I am certain of that creates a bad transcript is:

```Bash
help history
history -t transcript.txt
```

Edit:  The title and text changed to reflect that we need to figure out what is going on and to try to avoid jumping to a conclusion in regards to a potential solution.

issue_title: Create pipeline with an over-sampler follow by an under-sampler
issue_body:
There is a problem while trying to create a `Pipeline` with an over-sampler followed by an under-sampler and vice-versa.

This is due to the fact that the later sampler does not call `fit` after that the first sampler made a sampling.

We need to modified the pipeline such that `fit_sample` is called at sampling time if another sampler was used before.
@chkoar having a pass in `fit` would also solve the issue but I think that we can take the earlier solution for the moment.

issue_title: Invalid error handling.
issue_body:
# Summary
 1. Wrong approach for exception message updating.
 2. Useless error handling for invalid requests.
 3. Missing response handler for mult-part form request.

## Part 1
At the current moment implemented wrong approach for updating error massage like bellow:
```python
try:
    return json.loads(self.raw)["id"]
except KeyError as error:
    error.message += "Raw: {0}".format(self.raw)
    raise
```
Let's create a simple example `err.py` and test it on different versions of python:
```python
try:
    x = {"abc":123}["id"]
except KeyError as error:
    error.message += "Raw: {0}".format("some additional info")
    raise
```
Here is the output:
```bash
$ python2 err.py 
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "err.py", line 2, in <module>
    x = {"abc":123}["id"]
KeyError: 'id'
```
```bash
$ python3 err.py 
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "err.py", line 2, in <module>
    x = {"abc":123}["id"]
KeyError: 'id'

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "err.py", line 4, in <module>
    error.message += "Raw: {0}".format("some additiona info")
AttributeError: 'KeyError' object has no attribute 'message'
```
As you can see for `python2` there is no any additional info appeared. Moreover, `python3` exception does not have attribute with name `message` (chain exception).

## Part 2 (more serious)
__No exception appeared in case of invalid request!__
Let me show an example [err2.py.txt](https://github.com/reportportal/client-Python/files/999175/err2.py.txt)
Create save log request without time field like bellow:
```python
log_rq = SaveLogRQ(
    item_id=test.id,
    # time=timestamp(),
    message="Hello World!",
    level="INFO",
)
service.log(log_rq)
```
![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1073415/26032132/1e81abc2-3894-11e7-8f65-f04fd3969f99.png)

__If you run attached file, no error is appeared and no log message is created in the Report Portal!__

You will get error in case of trying to access `id` from response object for invalid save log request:
```python
log_rq = SaveLogRQ(item_id=test.id,
                                  # time=timestamp(),
                                  message="Hello World!",
                                  level="INFO")
r = service.log(log_rq)
print(r.id)
```
Traceback is following:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "err2.py.txt", line 49, in <module>
    print(r.id)
  File "./client-python/reportportal_client/model/response.py", line 17, in id
    return json.loads(self.raw)["id"]
KeyError: 'id'
```
## Part 3 (improvements)
Need to add response handler for multi-part form request, because json part represented as an `array`.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1073415/26032173/52732824-3895-11e7-9786-2c86b264e212.png)

issue_title: Talisker uses proxy address instead of client address for private endpoint filtering
issue_body:
Clients can access any private endpoint by spoofing the proxy header:

```bash
$ curl https://myservice/_status/metrics -H 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1'
# HELP some_metric
# TYPE some_metric counter
some_metric 1.0
```
I'm hitting taliskers's private endpoints behind a reverse proxy (which correctly sets in this case `X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1 <client.ip>`). `TALISKER_NETWORKS` is not set; though I believe that by setting `TALISKER_NETWORKS=<internal.network>` all private endpoints will be accessible by remote clients for this reason.

issue_title: Dependency management issue with requests
issue_body:
I am trying to get the Azure integration (https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/6261) finished and through CI but I am having a dependency management issue with the `requests` package during testing.

As you can see here https://travis-ci.org/certbot/certbot/jobs/408774623#L704, there seems to be an installation issue with the Azure libraries using an older version of `requests` and can't run the tests.

Does anyone have any ideas on how to fix this?

FWIW, the previous Azure PRs (from which this is based) didn't have this issue because they didn't have any tests.

issue_title: certbot timeout on fresh nginx install, requires manual "listen 443 ssl"
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):

Ubuntu Desktop 16.04.3

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):

apt-get, exactly as instructed at https://certbot.eff.org/#ubuntuxenial-nginx

## I ran this command and it produced this output:

```
sudo certbot --nginx certonly
```
...
```
IMPORTANT NOTES:
 - The following errors were reported by the server:

   Domain: [recated]
   Type:   connection
   Detail: Timeout

   To fix these errors, please make sure that your domain name was
   entered correctly and the DNS A/AAAA record(s) for that domain
   contain(s) the right IP address. Additionally, please check that
   your computer has a publicly routable IP address and that no
   firewalls are preventing the server from communicating with the
   client. If you're using the webroot plugin, you should also verify
   that you are serving files from the webroot path you provided.
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

I thought it would configure automatically for a fresh nginx installation with a simple vhost.  My vhost was a direct copy of the nginx example from nginx config file.

I fixed the problem by adding `listen 443 ssl` (just that, no certs) to the config and re-running `certbot --nginx`. certbot was then able to get in touch with the server and finish the installation.

I suggest that the certbot scripts ought to detect this case and add their own 443 line (and possibly `listen [::]:443`).

## Here is a Certbot log showing the issue (if available):
###### Logs are stored in `/var/log/letsencrypt` by default. Feel free to redact domains, e-mail and IP addresses as you see fit.

Redacted log attached.

## Here is the relevant nginx server block or Apache virtualhost for the domain I am configuring:

```
server {
	listen 80;
	listen [::]:80;
	server_name [reacted];
	root /var/www/[redacted];
	index index.html;
	location / {
		try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
	}
}
```

[letsencrypt.log.txt](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/files/1524901/letsencrypt.log.txt)

issue_title: acme standalone tests depend on port 1234 being free
issue_body:
...but it's not, causing the tests to fail.

The proper solution would be to bind to port 0, letting the OS choose a free port, then using getsockname to find out which port it was.

issue_title: Too many certificates already issued for dynamic dns provider sub domain
issue_body:
I get the following error trying to get a certificate for a subdomain of a dynamic dns provider:

    There were too many requests of a given type :: Error creating new cert :: Too many certificates already issued for: sytes.net
    Please see the logfiles in /var/log/letsencrypt for more details.

The domain name is like `abc.sytes.net`, the provider is http://www.noip.com/. And I would expect a lot of requests for such providers.

After digging trough some of the existing issues, I found the [public suffix list](https://publicsuffix.org/) as a possible solution.
Unfortunately it is missing some of the entries of dynamic dns providers that I used.

Would it make sense to provide an additional list only targeted on such sub domains, to allow an easier usage of letsencrypt?
Then we could just not apply the limit to them? At least the sub domain is owned by myself and the certificate is only usable for it, not for the whole domain `sytes.net`.

This issue is related to #1721, #2091 and #1834.

issue_title: documentation:  ./letsencrypt not equivalent to ./letsencrypt-auto
issue_body:
The documentation at https://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/using.html#id5 says:

> Throughout the documentation, whenever you see references to letsencrypt script/binary, you can substitute in letsencrypt-auto. For example, to get the help you would type:

That seems to imply that  ./letsencrypt and ./letsencrypt-auto are equivalent.  In fact they are not. 
1. I observe that  ./letsencrypt-auto is a useful executable script.
2. It is not a script/binary.  It is just a script.
3. ./letsencrypt is neither a script nor a binary.  It's a directory.

Specific suggestions:  
- Main point:  The documentation should be changed to eliminate references to ./letsencrypt in places where ./letsencrypt-auto is needed.
- Minor point:  documentation shouldn't say script/binary.  Perhaps the intent was to say "executable script".

issue_title: Fix assumptions around 'mode' for the pydatalab airflow operators
issue_body:
LoadOperator (table is always specified since it's a required parameter):
 - If table is not present, we should create it. In this case, it does not matter if mode is { create | append | overwrite }
 - If table is already present, we should throw if mode is 'create' because it's not clear if we should append or over-write. An argument can be made that it's always safe to append in this case, but this choice should be the user's. 

ExecuteOperator (table is an optional parameter): 
 - If table is specified, follow same behaviour as in LoadOperator.
 - If table is not specified, we should throw if mode is anything other than 'None'.

I've selected [**experiment.py**](https://github.com/PolyJIT/benchbuild/blob/52ec6f984169c158e9517fd6179ad4b6ded27eaf/benchbuild/experiment.py#L1-L185) for refactoring, which is a module of **98** lines of code that couples the top-level components tightly. Addressing this will make our codebase more maintainable and improve [Better Code Hub](https://bettercodehub.com)'s **Couple Architecture Components Loosely** guideline rating! 👍 

Here's the gist of this guideline:
- **Definition** 📖 
Minimize the amount of interface code; that is, code in modules that are both called from and call modules of other components ("throughput"), and code in modules that are called from modules of other components ("incoming").
- **Why**❓ 
Having loose coupling between top-level components makes it easier to maintain them in isolation.
- **How** 🔧 
You can hide a component's implementation details through various means, e.g. using the "abstract factory" design pattern.

You can find more info about this guideline in [Building Maintainable Software](http://shop.oreilly.com/product/0636920049159.do). 📖

issue_title: towards a general hosts.yml file
issue_body:
It would be good to have a `hosts.yml` that can be used by all users in an organization, which could even be put to somewhere like `/etc/sos.yml` so that users do not have to worry about host setup.

One major problem with a general `hosts.yml` file is username, because we have items such as

```
home: /home/USERID
```
in these files. It would be good to allow string interpolation in sos config files so that if we have such a file, we can simply do

```
sos config --global --set USERID myid
```
and then `USERID` would be used for other settings that use `USERID` (perhaps in a format `{USERID}`, or `${CONFIG["USERID"]}`. The latter might be possible right now if we make `CONFIG` available when the `hosts.yml` is used..

issue_title: Remote and local signatures.
issue_body:
We have not had any trouble in signatures because we always assumed a single file system. Currently if the other process (`task`) is handling the execution, the signature is passed to that process and that process is responsible for writing the signature.

Things have changed if we allow remote execution. Because local and remote files will be different, it is no longer possible to use the same signature. What we can do is use both remote and local signatures

1. The local process creates a signature
2. The remote process creates a signature
3. The remote process writes its own signature
4. The local process writes a local signature after it receives results from the remote server.

During re-evaluation, the local sos could skip a step (does not even know if the task is executed remotely) and the remote server could skip evaluation based on its own signature.

Bo

issue_title: Profiling ray.put performance.
issue_body:
Here are some instructions for profiling `ray.put` performance.

Build Arrow

```bash
# There are probably some other dependencies. See what is needed for Ray at http://ray.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install-on-ubuntu.html#.
git clone https://github.com/apache/arrow
cd arrow/cpp/
mkdir build

issue_title: SYN cookies cause errors
issue_body:
I am running an EC2 cluster using 18 nodes of type c4.8xlarge in the us-west-2c region.

After installing Ray and starting the cluster I find that a simple test script that launches 1000 tasks hangs. I see this error on the head node:

```
[FATAL] (/home/ubuntu/ray/src/photon/photon_client.c:53: errno: Resource temporarily unavailable) Check failure: type == EXECUTE_TASK

/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ray-0.0.1-py2.7.egg/core/src/photon/libphoton.so(photon_get_task+0xdd)[0x7fabb09e50db]
/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ray-0.0.1-py2.7.egg/core/src/photon/libphoton.so(+0x488c)[0x7fabb09e188c]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(PyEval_EvalFrameEx+0x82dc)[0x7fabbc156f3c]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(PyEval_EvalCodeEx+0x89e)[0x7fabbc1581ce]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(PyEval_EvalFrameEx+0x8596)[0x7fabbc1571f6]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(PyEval_EvalCodeEx+0x89e)[0x7fabbc1581ce]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(PyEval_EvalCode+0x32)[0x7fabbc1582e2]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(PyRun_FileExFlags+0xb0)[0x7fabbc178960]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(PyRun_SimpleFileExFlags+0xef)[0x7fabbc178b3f]
/opt/conda/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0(Py_Main+0xca4)[0x7fabbc18e484]
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(__libc_start_main+0xf0)[0x7fabbb384830]
python[0x400649]
```

Additionally, I see the following in `/var/log/syslog`:

```
Feb  9 05:27:29 ip-172-31-3-24 kernel: [11293.538325] TCP: request_sock_TCP: Possible SYN flooding on port 63148. Dropping request.  Check SNMP counters.
```

After some experimentation I found that turning off SYN cookies on the head node fixes the problem:
```
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies=0
sudo sysctl -p
```

We need to make sure that users do not encounter this issue. Perhaps some investigation will show how Ray can work with SYN cookies enabled. Alternatively we can update installation instructions to disable this kernel feature.

## Test Script
```
import ray

ray.init(redis_address="172.31.3.24:6379")

@ray.remote
def f():
    return ray.services.get_node_ip_address()

queries = [f.remote() for _ in range(1000)]
(ready, _) = ray.wait(queries, timeout=3000, num_returns=len(queries))
nodes = set(ray.get(ready))
print len(nodes), nodes
```

issue_title: Initialize horizontal grid externally to GFS
issue_body: Currently GFS has an internal representation for longitude and latitude which appear to never be put into the `state`, and the initialization code simply sets latitude and longitude to zero. Instead the initialization code should set a lat/lon grid, and GFS should run checks to make sure that the grid passed is as expected when it is called. Ideally on the first call, GFS would read the model state to determine the appropriate Fortran initialization arguments for the grid, and call those.

issue_title: Session handling in SSLStream
issue_body:
Python 3.6 added some APIs for session handling; for example, `wrap_bio` now takes a `session=` argument. We should expose these as well.

Also note that there may be a bug with session reuse when Python 3.6 is combined with openssl 1.1.0: https://github.com/openssl/openssl/issues/1550#issuecomment-287179654
It sounds like this can be worked around by making sure to always call `SSL_shutdown`. (We could do this and then not actually send the resulting bytes, even when `https_compatible=True`.)

issue_title: Stop generating CN fields
issue_body: Right now we throw some nonsense in the CN field and put the good stuff in the SAN, on the theory that this will help catch any old software that's incorrectly relying on the CN field instead of SAN. But this violates the CABF baseline requirements. We should leave the CN out entirely, which will let us catch broken software while also meeting the requirements.

- [x] Using the latest version of Twint.
- [x] I have searched the issues and there are no duplicates of this issue/question/request.

### Command Ran

import twint
c = twint.Config()
c.Username = "ferretscot"
c.Store_csv = True
c.Output = "ferretscot.csv"
twint.run.Profile(c)

***then***

import twint
c = twint.Config()
c.Username = "ferretscot"
c.Store_csv = True
c.Output = "ferretscot2.csv"
c.Profile_full = True
twint.run.Profile(c)

### Description of Issue
>Trying to get a comprehensive list of tweets and retweets for a given user. Twitters says example user given above has 7,292 tweets.

First command returns only 798 tweets and retweets (consistently). Earliest tweet returned is from 2017-10-07 (accounts first tweet was July 2013)

Second command is obviously much slower (30 minutes or so) but gets further back in time. However it also stops, this time at 2736 tweets, earliest tweet returned is from 2016-04-23.

Attempting to use Since and Until to collect earlier tweets than, for example, 2016-04-23, doesn't seem to work: it just begins returning data from present day.

### Environment Details
>MacOS High Sierra 10.13.5. Terminal. Python 3.6.5. pip twint module.

issue_title: Argument list too long error for large train set
issue_body:
This is more of a command line syntax question. 

I tried to train a model with 100K images. With all the training images in the same folder,
`calamari-train --files folder/*.png`
returns Argument list too long error because bash command cannot handle 100K arguments.

I then split the folder into 5 sub-folders with 20K images each. But 
`calamari-train --files folder1/*.png folder2/*.png folder3/*.png folder4/*.png folder5/*.png`
 still returns Argument list too long error. 

Is there a good way to pass the argument to --files to train calamari with large set of data?
